title: lonchocarpus necaxensis
intro: Lonchocarpus necaxensis, also known as the Mexican walnut or Jicara, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's seeds, leaves, and bark have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and cardiovascular protective effects, as well as the ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lonchocarpus necaxensis is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and it is also used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
